title: festuca ovina subsp. capillata
intro: Festuca ovina subsp. capillata, also known as sheep's fescue, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the genus Festuca. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Festuca ovina subsp. capillata include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Festuca ovina subsp. capillata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
